summary: Final closed-loop benchmark state after evaluator and guidance improvements.

models.notes: Needed clearer timing semantics and richer visible feedback, then converged.
Improved from early stalling to a clean closed-loop solve once feedback became actionable.
Converged after multiple visible eval cycles.
Solved on the first visible eval in the final packet.
Solved locally with more iterations than the 35B.
Stayed distracted by hidden-test hunting and never became a reliable closed-loop solver.
Failed for agent/tooling reasons before a valid benchmark solve; the same local family passed under pi.

takeaways: The benchmark is no longer mainly measuring harness confusion.
Concrete visible assertions, compiler errors, and integration symptoms materially improve weaker-agent performance.
Local models are viable when the agent runtime does not become the bottleneck.
A 9B local model can solve this task through the intended feedback loop.
